main_paper.raw: {"methods":["SceneLock: a reversible adversarial learning framework for data protection","Noise Serialization Encoding (NSE) module for generating adversarial perturbations","Noise Serialization Decoding (NSD) module for restoring original images","Integration of advanced image steganography for embedding perturbation values"],"problems":["Unauthorized access and misuse of camera-based autonomous driving datasets","Protection of intellectual property and privacy in autonomous driving data","Ensuring data is unusable for unauthorized AI models and manual annotation while remaining accessible to authorized users"],"datasets":["KITTI","nuScenes","Waymo"],"metrics":["Image quality degradation (for unauthorized users)","Restoration fidelity (for authorized users)","Effectiveness against unauthorized AI model performance","Manual annotation obstruction"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["First application of reversible adversarial perturbations for autonomous driving data protection","Introduction of a dual-module system (NSE and NSD) for reversible adversarial encoding and decoding","Integration of adversarial perturbations with image steganography for robust, reversible data protection","Ensures data is unusable for unauthorized users but fully recoverable for authorized users, maintaining data integrity and utility"]}

methods: SceneLock: a reversible adversarial learning framework for data protection
Noise Serialization Encoding (NSE) module for generating adversarial perturbations
Noise Serialization Decoding (NSD) module for restoring original images
Integration of advanced image steganography for embedding perturbation values
problems: Unauthorized access and misuse of camera-based autonomous driving datasets
Protection of intellectual property and privacy in autonomous driving data
Ensuring data is unusable for unauthorized AI models and manual annotation while remaining accessible to authorized users
metrics: Effectiveness against unauthorized AI model performance
novelty_claims: First application of reversible adversarial perturbations for autonomous driving data protection
Introduction of a dual-module system (NSE and NSD) for reversible adversarial encoding and decoding
Integration of adversarial perturbations with image steganography for robust, reversible data protection
Ensures data is unusable for unauthorized users but fully recoverable for authorized users, maintaining data integrity and utility

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["RAEDiff: a self-generation and self-recovery method for Reversible Adversarial Examples (RAEs) based on Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models (DDPM)","Adversarial Generative Process (AGP) to generate adversarial perturbations via DDPM without auxiliary information","Biased Gaussian Distribution (BGD) in DDPM to embed a distinctive watermark as a backdoor trigger","Dataset Training Performance Permission Management (DTPPM) to provide datasets with varying training performances for different requirements"],"problems":["Intellectual Property (IP) protection of image datasets against unauthorized use and malicious model training","Limitations of existing RAE methods that require embedding auxiliary information, which reduces adversarial capability and complicates restoration","Lack of mechanisms to provide datasets with varying training performance for different user requirements in Data Management Cloud Platforms (DMCP)"],"datasets":[],"metrics":[],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["First implementation of generating Reversible Adversarial Examples (RAEs) through self-generation and self-recovery using only a trained DDPM, without embedding auxiliary information","Unification of RAE generation and restoration by leveraging the Adversarial Generative Process (AGP) and DDPM prior knowledge","Introduction of Dataset Training Performance Permission Management (DTPPM) to enable datasets with varying training performance for different application scenarios and commercial value","Demonstration that RAEDiff can generate RAEs that protect DNN image datasets, ensuring availability for authorized users and preventing illegal usage by unauthorized users"]}

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Probabilistic and Geometric Depth (PGD): jointly leverages probabilistic depth uncertainty and geometric relationships across co-existed objects for accurate depth estimation","Probabilistic representation of instance depth: depth values are bucketed into intervals and depth is calculated as the expectation of the distribution, with uncertainty measured by top confidence scores","Geometric relation graph: constructs a graph across predicted objects to propagate and refine depth estimations using contextual relationships","Depth propagation guided by uncertainty: uses the uncertainty of each instance's depth to guide the propagation in the geometric relation graph","Implementation on FCOS3D monocular 3D object detector"],"problems":["Monocular 3D object detection from single images, which is ill-posed due to lack of direct depth information","Inaccurate instance depth estimation as the bottleneck for monocular 3D detection","Existing methods ignoring geometric relations across objects and not modeling depth uncertainty"],"datasets":["KITTI","nuScenes"],"metrics":["3D detection accuracy (e.g., AP3D on KITTI)","Speed/real-time efficiency"],"results":[{"metric":"KITTI 3D car detection benchmark (monocular vision-only)","value":"1st place among all monocular vision-only methods"},{"metric":"Speed","value":"Real-time efficiency maintained"}],"novelty_claims":["First to systematically show that inaccurate instance depth estimation is the bottleneck for monocular 3D detection and that the task can be simplified as instance depth estimation","Proposes a probabilistic representation for instance depth to capture uncertainty, which guides depth propagation","Introduces a geometric relation graph to model and leverage contextual relationships between objects for improved depth estimation","Demonstrates that a simple 2D detector, with depth-specific designs, can achieve state-of-the-art monocular 3D detection performance"]}

methods: Probabilistic and Geometric Depth (PGD): jointly leverages probabilistic depth uncertainty and geometric relationships across co-existed objects for accurate depth estimation

Probabilistic representation of instance depth: depth values are bucketed into intervals and depth is calculated as the expectation of the distribution, with uncertainty measured by top confidence scores
Geometric relation graph: constructs a graph across predicted objects to propagate and refine depth estimations using contextual relationships
Depth propagation guided by uncertainty: uses the uncertainty of each instance's depth to guide the propagation in the geometric relation graph
Implementation on FCOS3D monocular 3D object detector
problems: Monocular 3D object detection from single images, which is ill-posed due to lack of direct depth information
Inaccurate instance depth estimation as the bottleneck for monocular 3D detection
Existing methods ignoring geometric relations across objects and not modeling depth uncertainty

results.value: 1st place among all monocular vision-only methods

novelty_claims: First to systematically show that inaccurate instance depth estimation is the bottleneck for monocular 3D detection and that the task can be simplified as instance depth estimation
Proposes a probabilistic representation for instance depth to capture uncertainty, which guides depth propagation
Introduces a geometric relation graph to model and leverage contextual relationships between objects for improved depth estimation
Demonstrates that a simple 2D detector, with depth-specific designs, can achieve state-of-the-art monocular 3D detection performance
